rulings.Case Title: BRIAN ROBERTS et al VS. ALFA LAVAL INC SUED INDIVIDUALLLY AND AS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: ON ASBESTOS LAW AND MOTION CALENDAR FOR TUESDAY, DECEMBER 14, 2010 IN DEPARTMENT 220 AT 9:30 AM, LINE 23. Defendant Eaton Corp.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence creating a triable issue whether Mr. Roberts was exposed to any asbestos-containing products attributable to defendant. The objections to the declaration of Mr. Cohen are sustained. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: ROBERT MORGAN VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B*P) AS REFLECTED ON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 14, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 6. Defendant Crane Co.¿s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of production that plaintiffs do not possess and cannot reasonably obtain evidence that Mr. Morgan was exposed to asbestos-containing packing from original Crane Co. valves or that such exposure was a substantial factor contributing to his risk of developing an asbestos-related disease. Mr. Morgan¿s deposition and written discovery responses and the deposition testimony of Mr. McLean and Mr. Pantaleoni identified and discussed in plaintiff¿s discovery responses preclude defendant from successfully invoking the factually devoid prong of Aguilar. Even if defendant satisfied its burden of production, Mr. Morgan¿s declaration and defendant¿s responses to General Order 129 interrogatories create a triable issue whether Mr. Morgan was exposed to asbestos-containing Cranite. Defendant¿s objections to Mr. Morgan¿s declaration are overruled. = (220/HEK)

rulings.Case Title: JAMES HETZEL VS. ASBESTOS DEFENDANTS (B/P)AS REFLECTED ON EXHIBITS et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for Tuesday, December 14, 2010 in Department 220 at 9:30 a.m., Line 7. Defendant FDCC California, Inc.¿s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant satisfied its burden of production and plaintiff did not present admissible evidence that any debris he was exposed to that is attributable to defendant contained asbestos. The objections to Mr. Verhalen¿s deposition testimony and Mr. Ay¿s declaration are sustained. = (220/HEK)
